headline: Traditional Medicine Regulation Leaves 12,000 Practitioners Unregistered
context: Approximately 12,000 traditional and complementary medicine practitioners operate without formal registration as of 2025.

cards.big: 12,000 traditional medicine practitioners operate without registration or oversight in 2025
cards.sub: Traditional and Complementary Medicine Act 2016 implementation remains incomplete

cards.big: T&CM Act 2016 requires registration but enforcement began for only 3 of 7 practice categories
cards.sub: Malay traditional medicine Chinese medicine and homeopathy remain unregulated

cards.big: 48 adverse events from unregistered traditional treatments reported to MOH in 2025
cards.sub: Actual number estimated at 10 times higher due to severe underreporting

cards.big: China regulates all traditional practitioners through unified licensing since 2017 reform
cards.sub: Comprehensive registration protects patients while preserving traditional practice

cards.big: Respecting traditional medicine means ensuring its practitioners meet safety standards not exempting them
Should all traditional medicine practitioners be required to register within 2 years
